From what I remember, a fault adjacent mutant occurs when a small change in the code (a mutation) causes a nearby part of the code to fail, indicating that those parts are dependent on each other.

Considering fault adjacency, if two parts of the code are tightly coupled, a change in one should affect the other. Here, the mutant removed logic related to offsetting and rounding floor for time fields. This could impact how dates are handled around Daylight Saving Time transitions (as seen in testBug2182444_usCentral and others), which rely on accurate offset calculations.
